assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Removed `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` (tested deleted behavior); added `test_recommend_purls_dedup` + 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs; net +3 test functions; removal is justified as the qualifier-inclusion behavior no longer exists |' — MIXED verdict with both additive (test_recommend_purls_dedup, 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs) and reductive (removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers) signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Removed `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` (tested deleted behavior)' identifies the removal. Line 27: 'The removal of `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` is semantically justified (the qualifier-inclusion behavior was intentionally deleted), but the classification is MIXED because both additive and reductive structural signals are present.' — explicitly identifies the removal as a reductive structural signal.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 19 mentions 'test_recommend_purls_basic updated' but does not describe the specific assertion change (from fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to versioned PURL without qualifiers). The report does not characterize this assertion change as a relaxation or reductive signal. criterion-1.md references the new assertion value ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12') but does not compare it to the previous assertion or note the change as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'added `test_recommend_purls_dedup` + 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs' — explicitly recognizes 3 new test functions in the new test file as additive signals.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'added `test_recommend_purls_dedup`' — listed as an additive signal alongside the 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All are verification/analysis documents. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The report concludes with 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' confirming it is a read-only verification report.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents an 'Overall: WARN' verdict with 4 numbered 'Items requiring attention' for human review (lines 17-29). No merge action is taken or recommended. The report is structured as a verification comment for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: Summary table row '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | ...' with structural details (removed/added test functions, net count). Lines 27-28: Detailed analysis in 'Items requiring attention' section 3: 'The removal of `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` is semantically justified (the qualifier-inclusion behavior was intentionally deleted), but the classification is MIXED because both additive and reductive structural signals are present.' This provides both structural analysis (function additions/removals in table) and semantic assessment (justification of removal).

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 lists structural signals based on file content: 'Removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers' (function removal), 'added test_recommend_purls_dedup + 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs' (function additions), 'net +3 test functions' (function count comparison). Line 27 references 'the qualifier-inclusion behavior was intentionally deleted' which characterizes what the removed test function tested, derived from analyzing the test file content rather than quoting acceptance criteria. The analysis references specific test function names and counts from comparing the file state.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: WARN (two tests in purl_simplify.rs share identical structure with only data varying and could be parameterized); Test Documentation: PASS (all test functions have `///` doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A' — Eval Quality is explicitly N/A, and the Test Quality verdict is WARN (driven by Repetitive Test Detection), confirming Eval Quality does not affect the combination.
